examples: One way we keep track of the radiation budget is by looking at the albedo of the different surfaces on the planet.
A surface's albedo is the percentage of incoming solar energy, sunlight, that's reflected off that surface back into space.
Sea ice retreat and snow melt reduce Earth's albedo, which can lead to increased warmth and further melting.
When the sea ice melts you have water, which has an albedo of 0.2.
The Modis albedo measurements allow scientists to monitor how the Earth's reflectivity changes on a global scale.
